title: droseraceae
intro: The Droseraceae rank is a family of plants, specifically a group of flowering plants commonly known as the sundew family. This family consists of approximately 160 species of carnivorous plants, primarily found in bogs, marshes, and other wetland areas. The Droseraceae family is characterized by its unique ability to capture and digest insects for nutrients, with the most well-known genus within this family being Drosera, or the sundew. The sundew family is native to tropical and temperate regions around the world, with many species exhibiting distinctive leaves with sticky droplets to trap prey.
